zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Harding於1765年出生在美國維吉尼亞州蒙哥馬利時，她的父親Henry Harding III15歲，母親Rebecca Netherton15歲。她於1785年10月11日與William L. Hancock在美國維吉尼亞州申南多阿結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1825年在美國肯塔基州奧爾德姆享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: Harding
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Harding (Old English Hearding, literally ‘the hard one’ a derivative of Old English heard ‘hard, harsh, strong, firm, brave’). The surname was first taken to Ireland in the 15th century, and more families of the name settled there 200 years later in Tipperary and surrounding counties.

North German and Dutch: patronymic from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic compound personal names beginning with hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’, or a habitational name from a farm named Harding, of the same etymology.

History: Warren Gamaliel Harding (1865–1923), the 29th president of the US, was born on a farm in OH, of English and Scottish stock on his father's side. Early American bearers of this very common name include Joseph Harding who died at Plymouth in 1633. His great-great grandson Seth was a naval officer during the American Revolution.

variants.name: Hardin
meanings: French: from a pet form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed of the element hard ‘hard, strong’. This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Arduino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Francisco, Jose, Luis, Marina, Alberto, Alfonso, Alturo, Angel, Arturo, Azucena, Basilio, Carlos.  Italian Antonio, Gabino.
Italian and Hispanic: from the personal name Arduino, which is of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Harding 2.

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: Richmond Becomes the Capital
historicEvents.description: On April 18, 1780 Richmond became the capital of Virginia. It was the temporary capital from 1780-1788.
historicEvents.urlTitle: United States History- History of Richmond Virginia

historicEvents.description: The American Revolution ended with the surrender of Cornwallis at Yorktown Virginia.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: Wilderness Road Opens to Wagons
historicEvents.description: In 1796, the Wilderness Road opened up for wagon use. The route was used by colonial and early settlers to reach Kentucky from the East. It started in Virginia, and went southward to Tennessee and then went north to Kentucky. The main danger of this route was Native American attacks.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
War of 1812. U.S. declares war on Britain over British interference with American maritime shipping and westward expansion.

historicEvents.title: Monumental Church Built
historicEvents.description: The Monumental Church was built between 1812-1814 on the sight where the Richmond Theatre fire had taken place. It is a monument to those that died in the fire.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Harding was born in 1765, in Montgomery, Virginia, United States, her father, Henry Harding III, was 15 and her mother, Rebecca Netherton, was 15. She married William L. Hancock on 11 October 1785, in Shenandoah, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 7 daughters. She died in 1825, in Oldham, Kentucky, United States, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sarah Harding est née en 1765, à Montgomery, Virginie, États-Unis, son père, Henry Harding III, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Rebecca Netherton, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé William L. Hancock le 11 octobre 1785, à Shenandoah, Virginie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée en 1825, à Oldham, Kentucky, États-Unis, à l’âge de 60 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Harding(이)가 1765년 미국 버지니아 주 몽고메리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Harding III(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Rebecca Netherton(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1785년 10월 11일 미국 버지니아 주 셰난도에서 William L. Hancock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1825년 미국 켄터키 주 드함에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Jefferson Co., Kentucky: кэнтукки тоуншип, джеффирсон, Канзас, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Harding родилась в 1765 г. в местности монтгомири, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Henry Harding III было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Rebecca Netherton, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William L. Hancock 11 октября 1785 г. в местности шенандоах, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла в 1825 г. в местности олдхам, Кентукки, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 60 лет.
